perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may be canceled at any time without cause, by either party upon ninety (90) days written notice to the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be canceled at any time without cause, by either party upon thirty (30) days written notice to the other party.
perturbation.explanation: Reducing the notice period for termination could be problematic if it conflicts with reasonable expectations or implied duties.
perturbation.law_explanation: California Commercial Code Section 1203 implies a covenant of good faith and fair dealing in all contracts. A 30-day termination notice, especially if the distributor has made significant investments based on the agreement, might be deemed unreasonable and a violation of this implied covenant, as 90 days is already established in the original text as a more reasonable term.

GENERAL PROVISIONS [1101 - 1310] ( Division 1 enacted by Stats. 1963, Ch. 819. ) CHAPTER 2. General Definitions and Principles of Interpretation [1201 - 1206] ( Chapter 2 enacted by Stats. 1963, Ch. 819. ) 1203. (a) Whether a transaction in the form of a lease creates a lease or security interest is determined by the facts of each case. (b) A transaction in the form of a lease creates a security interest if the consideration that the lessee is to pay the lessor for the right to possession and use of the goods is an obligation for the term of the lease and is not subject to termination by the lessee, and: (1) the original term of the lease is equal to or greater than the remaining economic life of the goods; (2) the lessee is bound to renew the lease for the remaining economic life of the goods or is bound to become the owner of the goods; (3) the lessee has an option to renew the lease for the remaining economic life of the goods for no additional consideration or for nominal additional consideration upon compliance with the lease agreement; or (4) the lessee has an option to become the owner of the goods for no additional consideration or for nominal additional consideration upon compliance with the lease agreement. perturbation.justification: The modification reduces the notice period from 90 to 30 days, which the Law Explanation suggests might be unreasonable and violate the implied covenant of good faith, especially if the distributor has made significant investments. The original text already established 90 days as a more reasonable term. Snippet 1, referencing California Commercial Code Section 1203, implies a covenant of good faith and fair dealing, supporting the argument that a significantly reduced notice period could be deemed a contradiction. This aligns with the 'Inconsistencies Legal Contradiction' definition, as the altered time-sensitive obligation arguably violates state law regarding fair dealing, making the contractual term potentially unenforceable.
